office_does: The PUD commissioner governs electric and water rates, resource planning, reliability, conservation, and utility infrastructure.
race_blurb: Incumbent Jane Van Dyke faces utility engineer Gordon Matthews and utility systems worker Kevin Roegner. The rubric defines no candidate axes for the normalized Local category, so policy axes are omitted.

candidates.summary: A utility engineer emphasizing solar expansion, decarbonization, electrification, and new resources as hydro supply becomes constrained.
highlights: Supports expanded solar, utility partnerships, and redesigned community-solar programs [S1]
Says declining snowpack and constrained hydro supply require new resources [S1]

candidates.summary: A longtime utility worker prioritizing stable rates, financial oversight, grid resilience, cybersecurity, and controlled modernization.
highlights: Reports 34 years across utility line work, customer service, finance, and protection systems [S1]
Prioritizes rate stability, storm resilience, cybersecurity, and financial oversight [S1]

candidates.summary: The incumbent commissioner, emphasizing conservation, rate stability, customer assistance, and the utility's reliability record.
highlights: Supports conservation, efficiency, solar, nonprofit rates, and income-limited customer assistance [S1]
Reports one electric rate increase in 15 years and one water increase in 13 years [S1]
